title: fraxinus excelsior proles australis
intro: Fraxinus excelsior proles australis, also known as the southern ash, is a subspecies of the common ash tree (Fraxinus excelsior) native to the British Isles and parts of Europe. The bark and leaves of the southern ash have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of the southern ash include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, coughs, and respiratory issues. Southern ash can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
